sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, an 82-year-old male with a history of hypertension and stage three chronic kidney disease, presents for follow-up after an acute on chronic congestive heart failure exacerbation.
He reports noticing ankle swelling and shortness of breath, which prompted him to call 911.
The patient mentions he has been trying to watch his diet, but has been inconsistent with medication adherence, admitting to missing doses occasionally.
He also admits to consuming high-sodium foods, including pizza and chicken wings, on weekends.
The patient reports feeling good now, with improved symptoms since being discharged from the hospital.
Past medical history: The patient has hypertension and stage three chronic kidney disease.
Review of systems: The patient denies shortness of breath, chest pain, and problems sleeping since discharge.
He reports a good afternoon nap and no trouble getting up and moving around.
Current medications:
Bumex 2 milligrams daily for congestive heart failure
Cozaar 100 milligrams daily for hypertension
Norvasc 5 milligrams daily for hypertension
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 128/72, heart rate is 79, respiratory rate is 26, and oxygen saturation is 99%.
Physical exam: The patient's neck exam is unremarkable, with no jugular venous distention.
The heart exam reveals a stable 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.
The lungs are clear bilaterally.
The lower extremities show a trace of edema.
Test Results:
Echocardiogram shows a preserved EF of 55%, abnormal diastolic filling, and mild to moderate mitral regurgitation.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is a 82-year-old male with congestive heart failure, hypertension, and stage three chronic kidney disease.
Problem list: Congestive heart failure; Hypertension; Stage three chronic kidney disease; Mitral regurgitation.
Plan:
I prescribed Bumex 2 milligrams daily for congestive heart failure.
I instructed the patient to continue watching his diet and avoiding salty foods.
I want the patient to weigh himself daily and call if he gains 3 pounds in 2 days.
I ordered a renal artery ultrasound to rule out secondary causes of kidney disease.
I instructed the patient to continue on Cozaar 100 milligrams daily and Norvasc 5 milligrams daily for hypertension.
I recommended a follow-up appointment with a nutritionist for education on suitable foods.
Follow-up:
I will see the patient again in three months.
